entities.name: Alzheimer disease
entities.description: Complex heterogeneous neurodegenerative disorder classified as early onset or late onset
entities.evidence: Alzheimer's disease (AD) is a complex and heterogeneous neurodegenerative disorder

entities.name: APP
entities.description: Amyloid precursor protein gene involved in early onset AD
entities.evidence: Three main genes are involved in early onset AD: amyloid precursor protein (APP)

entities.name: PSEN1
entities.description: Presenilin 1 gene involved in early onset AD

entities.name: PSEN2
entities.description: Presenilin 2 gene involved in early onset AD

entities.name: APOE
entities.description: Apolipoprotein E gene; E4 allele is main risk factor for late-onset AD
entities.evidence: The apolipoprotein E (APOE) E4 allele has been found to be a main risk factor for late-onset Alzheimer's disease

entities.name: CLU
entities.description: Clusterin gene identified as potential AD risk factor by GWAS

entities.name: CR1
entities.description: Complement receptor 1 gene identified as potential AD risk factor

entities.name: PICALM
entities.description: Phosphatidylinositol binding clathrin assembly protein gene; potential AD risk factor

entities.name: SORL1
entities.description: Sortilin-related receptor gene; potential AD risk factor

entities.name: TREM2
entities.description: Triggering receptor expressed on myeloid cells 2; novel late-onset AD gene
entities.evidence: triggering receptor expressed on myeloid cells 2 (TREM2)

entities.name: CD33
entities.description: Cluster of differentiation 33; novel late-onset AD gene

entities.name: APOE E4
entities.description: E4 allele of APOE gene; main risk factor for late-onset AD
entities.evidence: The apolipoprotein E (APOE) E4 allele has been found to be a main risk factor

entities.name: neurodegeneration
entities.description: Progressive loss of neuronal structure and function

entities.name: early onset Alzheimer disease
entities.description: AD onset under 65 years of age
entities.evidence: classified as either early onset (under 65 years of age)

entities.name: late onset Alzheimer disease
entities.description: AD onset over 65 years of age
entities.evidence: or late onset (over 65 years of age)
